key_insight: A multi-level neural network that advances frequency-by-frequency can recover increasingly higher-order Fourier content in inverse scattering, making the reconstruction problem easier to optimize.
engineering_relevance: This framework offers a more robust way to solve inverse source and medium scattering tasks from multi-frequency measurements, with potential value in imaging and sensing pipelines.
method: Build a new neural-network level at each frequency step and use it to refine the current reconstruction.
Decompose one hard global inverse problem into a sequence of simpler local learning tasks.
Use higher-frequency data to progressively recover finer Fourier modes; NTK analysis supports this progression.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on inverse source scattering and inverse medium scattering, plus theoretical NTK analysis showing progressive recovery of higher-order Fourier components.
watch_item: The approach may depend on access to suitably ordered multi-frequency data and careful network training at each level, so performance could degrade if frequency coverage is limited or noisy.
paper_title: A Multi-Level Machine Learning Framework for Inverse Scattering Problems with Multi-Frequency Data
